message: there are no signs of either the bartender or customers. It's just me and the bartender.
The lights are dim, and no music is playing. Only the sight of the bartender silently polishing glasses in a terribly lonely scene.
It's not what you would call a clean shop.
He seems to be in his early 30s,
not dressed as flamboyantly as most of the customers.
He might be working non-stop from this hour until closing? He doesn't look like the kind of tough guy who could do that.
He's not particularly interested in me; he just seems to be looking this way out of obligation and making small talk.
Ah, you might not come here anymore.
A lot has happened in such a short time, huh?
That's what I've been thinking lately.
He quit. They say he's going back to his hometown where his relatives are. He used to hate the countryside that much, though.
Anyway, he won't be coming here anymore.
said he was going to take over his uncle's farm. Seems like he's giving up on his dreams.
Hey, did you actually know that Ariana was that talent?
Man, if I had known, I should've gotten her autograph or taken a picture. I never thought there would be a murder.
Life is so fragile. To think that a girl who was so bright and popular could die at the hands of a madman.
I can understand the feeling of wanting to move to the countryside.
Having faced such a huge setback at a young age
What will happen from now on
It got completely messed up because of.
Thinking about this makes me realize how cruel this world we live in is, and how fleeting humanity is.
Whenever I come to this shop, I can't help but remember the
sight of them when they were still lively and happy.
It's better not to come here again.
You take care of yourself too.
